column62: At Meineke, i was a floater, jumping from store to store all over Vegas covering managers days off at other stores. I would clock in to work and start my day by looking at the appointments people have made for the day in the office computer and count what other cars are still in the shop being worked on; checking names on the ROs to make sure if we are dealing insurance companies and negotiate pricing if needed. Call vendors for parts and delivery updates before stepping out and opening the garage doors so the technicians can come in. When the technicians were too busy working on customers cars with bigger issues, I would do the digital inspections and fluid changes/flushes. I was praised multiple times for giving the most thorough digital inspections and being able to push more sales by showing the customer the concern through pictures, in person, and explaining to them the issue so that they understand the severity of the situation.

column68: I’ve spent years following the local car culture in Las Vegas, and Frank’s has a reputation for the level of precision and specialized care that European vehicles actually require. I want to work somewhere where the standard is to do the job right the first time and where clear, honest communication with the client is the backbone of the business.
column69: The most important thing to me is a culture of technical accuracy and accountability. In a high-stakes environment where precision is the standard, I value having clear, reliable data and a team that takes ownership of the details to ensure the job is done right.  I also thrive in workplaces that prioritize clear communication—both within the team and with the client. I’ve found that when everyone is on the same page regarding expectations and timelines, it eliminates unnecessary friction and allows for a much more efficient, professional workflow.
column70:
In addition to my professional background, I’ve spent years performing hands-on maintenance and service on my own collection of classic and modern vehicles. I don't just understand the administrative side of a service desk; I have a practical, "under-the-hood" perspective on mechanical realities and shop timelines. This hands-on experience allows me to speak confidently with both technicians and customers because I understand the actual work being performed.

I’m also deeply connected to the local Las Vegas automotive community and regularly attend events to stay current on performance and enthusiast trends. I am looking to bring my experience managing high-volume operations and complex technical workflows to a team-oriented environment like Frank's. My goal is to use my combined organizational skills and mechanical aptitude to ensure every client receives the precise, expert service that European vehicle owners expect.
